title: Benchmarking distance choices
text: Mixed-type data determine unique dissimilarity. Different choices scaling, categorical comparisons, commensurability, associations can produce different geometries , consequently, different learning results. manydist provides three helpers studying choices systematically: all_dist_method_specs() constructs tables valid specifications; benchmark_mdist() evaluates mdist() every row compares successful distances; benchmark_comparisons() extracts pairwise diagnostics. guide shows define manageable candidate set, run safely, inspect failures, compare resulting dissimilarities, optionally assess clustering solutions. benchmark sensitivity-analysis tool; final choice still based intended downstream task appropriate validation design.

text: benchmark_comparisons() returns one row unique pair successful specifications. Four diagnostics always available: mad mean absolute difference corresponding pairwise dissimilarities; relative_distance divides MAD average magnitude two distances; mds_congruence compares classical MDS configurations; alienation 1−c2\sqrt{1-c^2}, cc MDS congruence. symmetric relative distance 2mean⁡(|da−db|)mean⁡(|da|)+mean⁡(|db|). \frac{2\operatorname{mean}(|d_a-d_b|)} {\operatorname{mean}(|d_a|)+\operatorname{mean}(|d_b|)}. zero identical distances depend many specifications included benchmark. Raw MAD retains differences absolute scale. Relative distance makes differences comparable, congruence alienation describe changes induced geometry. measures complementary rather competing rankings. autoplot() displays diagnostic annotated triangular heatmap. optional label column candidate_specs supplies axis labels. Relative distance Alienation

title: Benchmarking distance choices
text: Supplying cluster_k asks benchmark cluster every successful distance. value KK clustering method used within pairwise comparison. code: set.seed(2026)  penguin_folds <- rsample::vfold_cv(   penguin_train,   v = 5,   strata = species )  neighbor_grid <- tibble::tibble(   neighbors = c(1L, 3L, 5L, 7L, 9L, 15L) ) set.seed(2026)  knn_results <- tune::tune_grid(   knn_workflow,   resamples = penguin_folds,   grid = neighbor_grid,   metrics = yardstick::metric_set(yardstick::accuracy),   control = tune::control_grid(save_pred = TRUE) )  knn_metrics <- tune::collect_metrics(knn_results)  knn_metrics # A tibble: 6 × 7   neighbors .metric  .estimator  mean     n std_err .config       <int> <chr>    <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr> 1         1 accuracy multiclass 0.988     5 0.00487 pre0_mod1_post0 2         3 accuracy multiclass 0.988     5 0.00786 pre0_mod2_post0 3         5 accuracy multiclass 0.980     5 0.00886 pre0_mod3_post0 4         7 accuracy multiclass 0.988     5 0.00798 pre0_mod4_post0 5         9 accuracy multiclass 0.992     5 0.00485 pre0_mod5_post0 6        15 accuracy multiclass 0.996     5 0.00392 pre0_mod6_post0 knn_metrics |>   ggplot2::ggplot(     ggplot2::aes(       x = neighbors,       y = mean     )   ) +   ggplot2::geom_line(colour = "#2C7FB8") +   ggplot2::geom_point(size = 2, colour = "#2C7FB8") +   ggplot2::geom_errorbar(     ggplot2::aes(       ymin = mean - std_err,       ymax = mean + std_err     ),     width = 0.4   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = neighbor_grid$neighbors) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Number of neighbours",     y = "Cross-validated accuracy"   ) +   ggplot2::theme_minimal() best_neighbors <- tune::select_best(   knn_results,   metric = "accuracy" )  best_neighbors # A tibble: 1 × 2   neighbors .config       <int> <chr> 1        15 pre0_mod6_post0

title: manydist: distance-based learning with mixed-type data
text: distance representation produced step_mdist() can used downstream supervised learning workflows. illustrate nearest-neighbour classifier using distance--training representation. recipe first converts original mixed-type predictors distances observation training observations. model tuned number neighbours. example uses preset = "gower", workflow can used mdist() preset custom specification based method_cat, method_num, commensurable.
code: library(tidymodels)  set.seed(123)  penguin_split <- rsample::initial_split(   penguins_small,   prop = 0.75,   strata = species )  penguin_train <- rsample::training(penguin_split) penguin_test  <- rsample::testing(penguin_split)  set.seed(123)  penguin_folds <- rsample::vfold_cv(   penguin_train,   v = 5,   strata = species ) rec_knn <- recipes::recipe(species ~ ., data = penguin_train) |>   step_mdist(     recipes::all_predictors(),     preset = "gower",     output = "distance_to_training"   ) knn_spec <- nearest_neighbor_dist(   neighbors = tune::tune() ) |>   parsnip::set_mode("classification") knn_wf <- workflows::workflow() |>   workflows::add_recipe(rec_knn) |>   workflows::add_model(knn_spec)  knn_grid <- tibble::tibble(   neighbors = c(1, 3, 5, 7, 9) )  set.seed(123)  knn_res <- tune::tune_grid(   knn_wf,   resamples = penguin_folds,   grid = knn_grid,   metrics = yardstick::metric_set(accuracy) )  knn_res |>   tune::collect_metrics() # A tibble: 5 × 7   neighbors .metric  .estimator  mean     n std_err .config       <dbl> <chr>    <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr> 1         1 accuracy multiclass 0.996     5 0.00392 pre0_mod1_post0 2         3 accuracy multiclass 1         5 0       pre0_mod2_post0 3         5 accuracy multiclass 0.992     5 0.00485 pre0_mod3_post0 4         7 accuracy multiclass 0.992     5 0.00485 pre0_mod4_post0 5         9 accuracy multiclass 0.988     5 0.00798 pre0_mod5_post0 best_knn <- knn_res |>   tune::select_best(metric = "accuracy")  best_knn # A tibble: 1 × 2   neighbors .config       <dbl> <chr> 1         3 pre0_mod2_post0 final_knn_wf <- knn_wf |>   tune::finalize_workflow(best_knn)  final_knn_fit <- final_knn_wf |>   parsnip::fit(data = penguin_train)  knn_pred <- predict(final_knn_fit, new_data = penguin_test) |>   dplyr::bind_cols(penguin_test |> dplyr::select(species))  yardstick::accuracy(knn_pred, truth = species, estimate = .pred_class) # A tibble: 1 × 3   .metric  .estimator .estimate   <chr>    <chr>          <dbl> 1 accuracy multiclass     0.988

title: On the use of presets
text: presets mainly convenient shortcuts combinations mdist() arguments. example, "u_dep" preset corresponds unbiased association-aware distance construction using categorical association information, numerical preprocessing, commensurability. available presets can listed dist_methods_tbl(). engine column helps distinguish presets implemented directly manydist presets based external constructions ports. example, "u_indep", "u_dep", "u_mix", "hl" implemented directly manydist, whereas "dkss" uses kdml implementation "gudmm" "mod_gower" external ports.
code: dist_methods_tbl() |>   dplyr::filter(argument == "preset", method != "custom") |>   dplyr::select(     preset = method,     response_aware,     engine   ) |>   kableExtra::kbl()

title: On the use of presets
text: Consider "u_dep" preset, computes unbiased association-aware distance measure. result shows , case, preset equivalent corresponding explicit specification method_cat, method_num, commensurable.
code: x_penguins <- penguins_small |>   dplyr::select(-species)  d_u_dep_preset <- mdist(   x_penguins,   preset = "u_dep" )  d_u_dep_explicit <- mdist(   x_penguins,   method_cat = "tvd",   method_num = "pc_scores",   commensurable = TRUE )  all.equal(   as.matrix(d_u_dep_preset$distance),   as.matrix(d_u_dep_explicit$distance) ) [1] TRUE

what: Presets based on specific distance constructions
title: On the use of presets
text: presets merely combinations basic arguments . Instead, correspond specific distance constructions, implementations, external ports. example, "mod_gower" preset computes modified version Gower’s distance. Similarly, presets "gower", "gudmm", "dkss" refer specific distance constructions. cases, preset identifies distance method , rather simply selecting combination method_cat, method_num, commensurable. "euclidean" preset slightly different: uses dedicated preprocessing strategy, converting categorical variables one-hot dummy variables computing Euclidean distances column-wise standardized dataset. can compare standard Euclidean distance obtained dist() numerical variables , provided numerical variables standardized first. default remains method_num = "std", numerical-Euclidean preset agrees dist() applied standardization. predictors numeric, however, method_num can overridden. example, method_num = "none" skips numerical preprocessing reproduces ordinary Euclidean distance original variables. numerical-inputs, supported choices "none", "std", "range", "robust", "pc_scores". override restricted numerical-data. categorical variables present, "euclidean" preset retains standard preprocessing strategy: categorical variables one-hot encoded method_num = "std" used.
code: d_mod_gower <- mdist(   x_penguins,   preset = "mod_gower" )  d_mod_gower MDist object   preset : mod_gower   number of observations : 333   number of continuous variables   : 4   number of categorical variables   : 2 x_penguins_num <- penguins_small |>   dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))  d_euc_mdist <- mdist(   x_penguins_num,   preset = "euclidean" )  d_euc_dist <- dist(   x_penguins_num |>     dplyr::mutate(       dplyr::across(         dplyr::everything(),         ~ as.numeric(scale(.x))       )     ),   method = "euclidean" )  all.equal(   as.matrix(d_euc_mdist$distance),   as.matrix(d_euc_dist) ) [1] TRUE d_euc_unscaled <- mdist(   x_penguins_num,   preset = "euclidean",   method_num = "none" )  d_euc_dist_unscaled <- dist(   x_penguins_num,   method = "euclidean" )  all.equal(   as.matrix(d_euc_unscaled$distance),   as.matrix(d_euc_dist_unscaled) ) [1] TRUE

text: function compare_lovo_mdist() compares leave-one-variable-diagnostics across several distance specifications. title: Distance-Based Learning for Mixed-Type Data
text: main function manydist mdist(). computes dissimilarities numerical, categorical, mixed-type data. mixed-type dissimilarity can computed preset. example, preset = "gower" computes Gower-type dissimilarity. presets can use response variable. response supplied, used response-aware distance construction treated ordinary predictor. Custom specifications can defined combining categorical method, numerical preprocessing method, commensurability rule.
code: penguins_small <- penguins |>   select(-year) |> drop_na() D <- penguins_small |> select(-species) |>    mdist(preset = "gower")  as.matrix(D$distance)[1:5,1:5] |> round(digits=2) ##      1    2    3    4    5 ## 1 0.00 0.21 0.25 0.24 0.07 ## 2 0.21 0.00 0.07 0.09 0.25 ## 3 0.25 0.07 0.00 0.06 0.26 ## 4 0.24 0.09 0.06 0.00 0.23 ## 5 0.07 0.25 0.26 0.23 0.00 D_resp <- penguins_small |> mdist(   response = species,   preset = "u_dep" )  D_resp ## MDist object ##   preset : u_dep  ##   number of observations : 333  ##   number of continuous variables   : 4  ##   number of categorical variables   : 2  ##   parameters: ##     - categorical method: tvd ##     - numerical preprocessing: pc_scores ##     - commensurability adjustment: TRUE D_custom <- mdist(penguins_small |> select(-species),                   preset = "custom",                   method_cat = "matching",                   method_num = "std",                   commensurable = TRUE )  D_custom ## MDist object ##   preset : custom  ##   number of observations : 333  ##   number of continuous variables   : 4  ##   number of categorical variables   : 2  ##   parameters: ##     - categorical method: matching ##     - numerical preprocessing: std ##     - commensurability adjustment: TRUE ##     - number of principal components: 6 ##     - inertia threshold: NULL

what: Benchmark and compare multiple `mdist()` specifications — benchmark_mdist
title: Benchmark and compare multiple `mdist()` specifications — benchmark_mdist
text: Applies [mdist()] repeatedly tibble distance-method specifications, typically generated [all_dist_method_specs()]. compares every pair successful distance specifications using distance, configuration, optional clustering diagnostics.

title: Benchmark and compare multiple `mdist()` specifications — benchmark_mdist
code: benchmark_mdist(   x,   response = NULL,   specs = all_dist_method_specs(),   dims = 2,   cluster_k = NULL,   cluster_methods = c("pam", "hclust", "spectral"),   hclust_method = "average",   spectral_sigma = NULL,   spectral_nstart = 50 )

title: Benchmark and compare multiple `mdist()` specifications — benchmark_mdist
text: x data frame tibble predictors, optionally including response column. response Optional response column inside `x`, supplied either unquoted character string. specs tibble method specifications. default, generated [all_dist_method_specs()]. must contain columns `spec_type`, `preset`, `method_cat`, `method_num`, `commensurable`. optional `label` column supplies display labels comparisons plots. dims Integer. Number dimensions used classical multidimensional scaling computing congruence alienation coefficients. cluster_k Optional integer. Number clusters used pairwise adjusted Rand indices. `NULL`, clustering performed. cluster_methods Character vector specifying clustering methods used `cluster_k` supplied. Possible values `"pam"`, `"hclust"`, `"spectral"`. hclust_method Character string specifying linkage method passed [stats::hclust()] `"hclust"` requested. spectral_sigma Optional numeric value Gaussian affinity bandwidth used spectral clustering. `NULL`, default used [spectral_dist()] applied. spectral_nstart Integer. Number random starts used k-means step spectral clustering.

title: Benchmark and compare multiple `mdist()` specifications — benchmark_mdist
text: object class `"MDistBenchmark"`, also tibble.   contains supplied specifications together : result corresponding output [mdist()], error object   specification failed. ok Logical indicator; `TRUE` run completed successfully,   `FALSE` otherwise. error Error message failed runs, `NA` otherwise. Use [benchmark_comparisons()] obtain pairwise diagnostics [ggplot2::autoplot()] draw annotated triangular heatmap.

title: Benchmark and compare multiple `mdist()` specifications — benchmark_mdist
text: row `specs` interpreted one valid `mdist()` configuration. Preset-based custom component-based specifications supported. Failed specifications caught returned output rather stopping full benchmark. Preset specifications use `preset` column ignore `method_cat`, `method_num`, `commensurable`. Component specifications evaluated `preset = "custom"` use `method_cat`, `method_num`, `commensurable`. Pairwise mean absolute difference (`mad`) computed lower triangle dissimilarity matrix. symmetric relative distance defined $$   \frac{2\,\mathrm{mean}(|d_a-d_b|)}        {\mathrm{mean}(|d_a|)+\mathrm{mean}(|d_b|)}. $$ Classical multidimensional scaling configurations compared using [congruence_coeff()]. corresponding alienation coefficient \(\sqrt{1-c^2}\). `cluster_k` supplied, requested clustering method applied every successful distance specification. Partitions produced clustering method compared pairwise using adjusted Rand index. `cluster_k = NULL`, clustering performed ARI columns included comparisons.

title: Benchmark and compare multiple `mdist()` specifications — benchmark_mdist
code: if (requireNamespace("palmerpenguins", quietly = TRUE)) {   data("penguins", package = "palmerpenguins")    penguins_small <- palmerpenguins::penguins |>     dplyr::select(       species, bill_length_mm, bill_depth_mm, flipper_length_mm,       body_mass_g, island, sex     ) |>     tidyr::drop_na()    specs <- all_dist_method_specs(     mode = "presets_only",     preset = c("gower", "u_indep", "u_dep")   )    res <- benchmark_mdist(     penguins_small,     response = species,     specs = specs   )    res |>     dplyr::select(spec_type, preset, ok, error)    benchmark_comparisons(res)   ggplot2::autoplot(res, metric = "relative_distance") } #> Warning: For method(s) 'matching', category dissimilarities do not depend on conditional profiles; `response` was therefore ignored.

code: methods = list(   gower = list(preset = "gower"),   u_dep = list(preset = "unbiased_dependent"),   custom = list(     preset = "custom",     method_cat = "matching",     method_num = "std",     commensurable = TRUE   ) )

title: Compare LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications — compare_lovo_mdist
text: distance specification, function: Computes full mixed-type distance using mdist(). Recomputes distance repeatedly leaving one variable time. Measures impact variable using metrics mean absolute deviation (MAD), congruence-based diagnostics, , requested, clustering-based agreement measures. results combined across methods returned MDistLOVOCompare object, supports print(), summary(), ggplot2::autoplot().

title: Compare LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications — compare_lovo_mdist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(manydist) library(palmerpenguins)  data <- penguins |>   dplyr::select(-species) |>   tidyr::drop_na()  cmp <- compare_lovo_mdist(   x = data,   methods = list(     gower = list(preset = "gower"),     u_dep = list(preset = "unbiased_dependent")   ),   cluster_k = 3 )  summary(cmp) autoplot(cmp, metric = "mad_importance") autoplot(cmp, metric = "pam_importance") } # }

title: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
text: L1 numeric matrix data frame (rows = observations) L2 numeric matrix data frame number rows L1

title: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
text: scalar \([-1,1]\) measuring similarity two configurations.

title: Congruence coefficient between two configurations — congruence_coeff
text: congruence coefficient defined $$   \frac{\langle D_1, D_2 \rangle_F}        {\sqrt{\langle D_1, D_1 \rangle_F \langle D_2, D_2 \rangle_F}} $$ \(D_1\) \(D_2\) pairwise distance matrices derived L1 L2.

title: Leave-one-variable-out diagnostics for distance-based variable importance — lovo_mdist
text: `lovo_mdist()` useful assessing strongly predictor contributes distance-based representation. predictor considered influential removing produces large change dissimilarity matrix, multidimensional scaling configuration, optional clustering partition. returned object contains several LOVO diagnostics. main distance contribution measured mean absolute difference full dissimilarity matrix leave-one-variable-matrix (`mad_importance`). normalized version stored `relative_distance`. function also compares classical multidimensional scaling configurations computed full leave-one-variable-dissimilarities. diagnostics stored `mds_congruence` / `cc_importance` `ac_importance`, latter corresponding alienation coefficient. `cluster_k` supplied, function additionally computes clustering partitions full leave-one-variable-dissimilarities compares using adjusted Rand index. corresponding importance measures defined `1 - ARI` stored `pam_importance`, `hclust_importance`, `spectral_importance`, depending selected clustering methods. Columns can numeric, factors, mixture . new_data Optional data frame matrix containing new observations. supplied, distances computed rows `new_data` rows `x`, producing rectangular test--training dissimilarity matrix. response Optional response variable used response-aware categorical dissimilarities. can supplied unquoted column name character string. response column removed predictors computing distances. method_cat Character string specifying categorical-variable dissimilarity used `preset = "custom"`. Common values include `"matching"` `"tvd"`. Use [all_dist_method_specs()] inspect available methods. method_num Character string specifying numerical-variable preprocessing. Supported values `"none"` preprocessing, `"std"` standard-deviation scaling, `"range"` range scaling, `"robust"` inter-quartile-range-based scaling, `"pc_scores"` principal-component score scaling. argument used directly `preset = "custom"`. `preset = "euclidean"` predictors numeric, can also override default `"std"` preprocessing; particular, `"none"` gives ordinary Euclidean distance original variables. commensurable Logical. `TRUE`, dissimilarities scaled average contribution variable overall distance equal 1. ncomp Integer `NULL`. Number principal components retain `method_num = "pc_scores"`. `NULL`, available components used unless `threshold` supplied supported underlying method. threshold Numeric `NULL`. Optional cumulative variance threshold used `method_num = "pc_scores"`. preset Character string specifying predefined distance specification. Available values include `"custom"`, `"gower"`, `"unbiased_dependent"`, `"u_dep"`, `"u_indep"`, `"u_mix"`, `"hl"`, `"gudmm"`, `"dkss"`, `"mod_gower"`, `"euclidean"`. `preset` `"custom"`, arguments `method_cat`, `method_num`, `commensurable`, `interaction` normally handled preset user-supplied values ignored. exception `method_num` `preset = "euclidean"` predictors numeric. interaction Logical. `TRUE`, adds interaction-aware continuous-categorical component based local predictive separability. prop_nn Numeric. Proportion nearest neighbours used `interaction = TRUE`. score Character string specifying score used `interaction = TRUE`. Available values include `"ba"` balanced accuracy `"logloss"`. decision Character string specifying decision rule used `score = "ba"`. default `"prior_corrected"`. gower_average Logical; used `preset = "gower"`. `TRUE`, returns standard Gower dissimilarity averaged variables, matching scale [cluster::daisy()] `metric = "gower"`. `FALSE`, returns sum per-variable Gower contributions, equivalent multiplying averaged Gower dissimilarity number active variables.

Square train-train dissimilarities stored   `"dissimilarity"`/`"dist"` objects; rectangular test--training   dissimilarities stored `"dissimilarity"`/`"matrix"` objects.

title: Mixed-type dissimilarities for distance-based learning — mdist
text: `mdist()` main distance-construction function `manydist`. can return ordinary train-train dissimilarities rectangular test--training dissimilarities `new_data` supplied. resulting object stores dissimilarity matrix metadata distance specification used. `preset = "custom"`, users manually choose numerical preprocessing, categorical dissimilarity, commensurability, optional interaction term. `"gower"` preset follows usual Gower construction based range scaling continuous variables matching dissimilarities categorical variables. `gower_average` argument controls whether result averaged variables returned sum variable-wise contributions. #' `"u_dep"`, `"unbiased_dependent"`, `"u_indep"`, `"u_mix"` presets convenience specifications unbiased commensurable mixed-variable dissimilarities. `"euclidean"` preset computes Euclidean distance standardizing numerical variables one-hot encoding standardizing categorical variables. numerical-inputs, `"std"` remains default, `method_num` can overridden. Setting `method_num = "none"` produces numerical distances [stats::dist()] applied directly original variables. `"gudmm"`, `"dkss"`, `"mod_gower"` presets provide additional mixed-type distance constructions. presets currently support train-train distances stop `new_data` supplied. Use [all_dist_method_specs()] inspect available distance components method specifications.

text: recipe recipe object. ... Selector(s) predictor columns used [mdist()]. passed [recipes::recipes_eval_select()] preparation. role Role new distance columns. default `"predictor"`. trained Logical recipes internals. set manually. output Character string specifying type distance output. `"distance_to_training"` returns distances baked data training observations usual choice prediction workflows. `"pairwise"` returns within-training pairwise dissimilarity matrix intended training-distance-based clustering workflows. response_used Logical. `TRUE` (default) recipe exactly one outcome, response-aware distance specifications use outcome step prepared. Set `FALSE` construct distance predictors . Specifications response-aware never use outcome. preset Character string specifying distance preset passed [mdist()]. Available values include `"custom"`, `"gower"`, `"unbiased_dependent"`, `"u_dep"`, `"u_indep"`, `"u_mix"`, `"hl"`, `"gudmm"`, `"dkss"`, `"mod_gower"`, `"euclidean"`. Supported values `"none"` preprocessing, `"std"` standard-deviation scaling, `"range"` range scaling, `"robust"` inter-quartile-range-based scaling, `"pc_scores"` principal-component score scaling. `preset = "custom"`, `NULL` defaults `"none"`. `preset = "euclidean"`, default `"std"`; predictors selected step numeric, explicitly supplied value can override default. commensurable Logical. `TRUE`, dissimilarities scaled average contribution variable overall distance equal 1, supported selected distance specification. ncomp Integer `NULL`. Baking genuinely new data supported mode, output intended training-clustering workflows [pam_dist()] [spectral_dist()].

title: manydist 0.5.1
text: Added wdi_2022, documented snapshot selected 2022 World Development Indicators reproducible mixed-type distance examples.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-09

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Expanded manydist package focused mixed-type distance construction broader framework distance-based learning mixed-type data. Updated package title description reflect support distance construction, distance-based modelling workflows, variable-importance diagnostics, clustering. Changed package maintainer Angelos Markos Alfonso Iodice D’Enza.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added revised mdist() interface documentation mixed-type distance construction. Added support additional mixed-type distance specifications presets. Added response-aware distance construction tools supervised mixed-type workflows. Added interaction-aware distance components continuous-categorical relationships. Added helper infrastructure preprocessing applying mixed-type distance specifications consistently across training new data. Added utilities generating benchmarking distance-method specifications.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added step_mdist() integrating manydist distances recipes tidymodels workflows. Added nearest_neighbor_dist() related prediction functions nearest-neighbour models based precomputed manydist-generated distances. Added pam_dist() partitioning around medoids using manydist dissimilarities. Added spectral_dist() spectral_from_dist() spectral clustering distance matrices. Added support functions converting distances affinities fitting distance-based clustering models.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added lovo_mdist() leave-one-variable-diagnostics distance matrices. Added compare_lovo_mdist() lovo_method_spec() comparing LOVO diagnostics across multiple distance specifications. Added congruence- alienation-based diagnostics comparing multidimensional scaling configurations. Added optional clustering-based LOVO diagnostics using PAM, hierarchical clustering, spectral clustering.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added gen_mixed() generate_dataset() generating mixed-type example simulation data. Added benchmark_mdist() benchmarking distance specifications across datasets method grids. Added all_dist_method_specs() distance-method metadata helpers.

title: manydist 0.5.0
text: Added documentation new modelling, clustering, LOVO, benchmarking, recipe functions. Updated package-level description metadata 0.5.0 release. Removed CRAN-inappropriate development files, caches, vignette outputs source build.
